findings.source: generate unit — Gemini *url.Error API key leakage (security gate BLOCK)
findings.description: Go's http.Client.Do wraps transport failures in *url.Error whose .Error() includes the full URL. For Gemini's ?key= auth, this silently leaks the API key on DNS/TLS/timeout errors. Fixed with sanitiseHTTPError stripping the URL, preserving only Op + cause.
findings.proposedRule: P12: Sanitise *url.Error for query-param auth — Go embeds full URL in transport errors, leaking ?key= credentials.

findings.source: generate unit — PR review: all 3 providers read body before status check
findings.description: All providers consumed 256KB via io.ReadAll before checking resp.StatusCode. Non-200 responses allocate memory unnecessarily. Fixed: check status first, drain body for connection reuse, read only on 200.
findings.proposedRule: P13: Check HTTP status before reading body — drain on error for connection reuse, avoid allocating error bodies.

findings.source: generate unit — PR review CRITICAL: outputManifestResult missing os.WriteFile
findings.description: Success message said 'written to X' but os.WriteFile was never called. All tests used --dry-run, so the write path was untested. Fixed by adding WriteFile and tests asserting file existence.
findings.proposedRule: P14: Test the write path, not just dry-run — if all tests use --dry-run, the os.WriteFile path is untested.

findings.source: generate unit — cascade from P14 fix: non-dry-run tests wrote to working directory
findings.description: When the missing WriteFile was added, existing non-dry-run tests would have written to the repo working directory. Updated all to use t.TempDir() + filepath.Join for hermetic output paths.
findings.proposedRule: P15: Non-dry-run tests must use t.TempDir() — writing to working dir pollutes repo and breaks CI sandboxes.

findings.source: generate unit — PR review: doubled provider prefix in error messages
findings.description: sanitisedHTTPError.Error() included provider name, but callers wrapped with fmt.Errorf('gemini: send request: %w', ...), producing 'gemini: send request: gemini: Post: <cause>'. Fixed by removing provider field from the wrapper type.
findings.proposedRule: P16: Error wrapper types should not duplicate caller context — wrapper provides its own context, caller provides outer.

findings.source: generate unit + tui unit — PR reviewers caught real bugs all 5 gates missed
findings.description: Reinforces existing P10. Generate PR: missing os.WriteFile (functional regression). Tui PR: lineTracker applied unconditionally (would garble input in non-accessible mode). Both passed all automated gates.
findings.proposedRule: P10 (reinforced): PR review catches semantic bugs that gates miss.
